description: Evaluation briefs for desktop-ui-mastery. Run each with and without the skill installed, blind-compare outputs, and grade against the assertions. Substantive briefs on purpose: trivial prompts do not trigger skills.
grading: A written point of view accompanies the design and could not describe a competitor unchanged
Desktop anatomy present (no centered column; correct density; status/persistence thinking)
Full keyboard path and visible focus for the primary loop
Tabular figures in numeric columns; accent used on a handful of elements
Not recognizable as any specific existing product's screen
At least one design element that exists in no reference file (novelty ledger)

briefs.prompt: Design and build the main dashboard for a desktop app that monitors a delivery fleet of 300 vehicles: live status, exceptions, fuel, and route adherence. Ops staff live in this screen all day.
Build the claims-processing workspace for an insurance back-office desktop tool. Assessors process 80-120 claims a day; speed and keyboard flow matter more than anything.
Design a desktop editor for a food magazine's recipe database: structured fields, images, versioning, editorial review states.
Design the position overview and transfer flow for a corporate treasury desktop app. Money moves here; trust and comprehension outrank charm.
Build a desktop research-notes tool for a PhD student: capture, link, and retrieve thousands of notes and PDFs. Should feel like a thinking space, not a database.
Add a command palette to an existing project-management desktop app with 60 actions across projects, tasks, people, and views. Specify behavior fully and build it.
Design the settings surface for a file-sync desktop app: account, sync rules, bandwidth, conflicts, notifications, and a safe path to unlinking a machine.
Design a clipboard-history utility for desktop power users. It should barely exist until summoned.
Design the first-run experience for a desktop analytics tool so a new user reaches their first real insight from their own data inside five minutes.
Review this dashboard implementation for design quality and tell me exactly what to fix. [attach any generated dashboard HTML, or use evals/fixtures if you add them]
This admin panel looks AI-generated. Strip the generic tells without changing its features. [attach generated admin panel code]
Imagine the future of desktop email for someone who receives 300 messages a day. Break one assumption, keep it learnable, and show me the ladder back to something buildable this year.
Imagine what a desktop IDE looks like when an AI writes 80% of the code. Where does the human's attention and authorship live?
Create the complete design token system for a desktop app for marine cargo insurers: calm, dense, trustworthy. Explain every derivation.
